what_it_does: This file is a channel attachment metadata record that links a design-source channel entry to an image asset (deck1-2026/p-11) and records when it was added.
observations: It contains only YAML frontmatter and no body content
It references an image target using a custom image:// URI scheme
The filename id matches the id field: att-6636aec8
standouts: Custom image:// URI target pointing to design-source/deck1-2026/p-11
Attachment acts as a typed bridge between a channel memory entry and an external image asset
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, channel, attachment_type, target, added fields
Follows a naming convention where the filename repeats the attachment id
Uses a URI-style target rather than a filesystem path
concerns: No descriptive title or caption for the image
No author or provenance metadata beyond a timestamp
Target URI scheme is not further defined in this file
notes: This is a minimal attachment stub that records an image reference for a design-source channel entry.
questions: What does the image:// URI resolve to in storage?
Is p-11 a slide, page, or specific crop within deck1-2026?
Who added this attachment and why?
purpose_vs_actual: The implied purpose (record an image attachment for a channel) matches the content; no explicit purpose statement is present.
apparent_themes: Separation of content metadata from binary assets
intention_signals: It appears intended to create a durable, typed link between a design-source channel record and a specific image asset, with an audit timestamp.
novelty: Use of a custom image:// URI scheme for target addressing rather than a relative file path or URL.
contribution: It contributes a concrete image attachment reference with channel context and a creation timestamp, which a fused system would keep as evidence of which image belonged to a design-source entry at a specific time.
summary_for_embedding: A channel attachment metadata file in the design-source channel that records an image target (image://design-source/deck1-2026/p-11) and an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: To link a design-source channel memory entry to a specific image asset and record when the link was created.
